Elsewhere in London, Paul traces a great, great grandmother on his father's side of the family who was sent to prison for her part in an assault(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist Emma and Steve, whose front garden in Hoveringham overlooking the River Trent, feels very much 'on display' to passers-by. programme.desc: The younger son of George V struggles to cope with an uncontrollable stammer, prompting his wife to enlist the aid of an eccentric Australian speech therapist. programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In the final episode of the series, the six baby animals approach the end of their first year on Earth. In Iceland, an Arctic fox cub rejected by his mother must learn where to find food for the first time, while in Kenya, spotted hyena twin sisters must roam a territory they share with the biggest threat to their lives, lions. Whether breaking ties or building bonds, all the young animals must learn for themselves how to stay healthy and safe(n)

programme.desc: Michael Buerk and the team locate an heir living in one of the most famous addresses in the world, and also track down family members who could inherit a substantial windfall(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A British chopper pilot rushes to the aid of an elderly farmer who is fighting for his life, while two UK policemen discover a darker side to life down under(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore food-related customs associated with wedding ceremonies. The duo help prepare a Scottish breakfast fit for a bride and groom, and highlight traditions behind the star of the nuptial feast - the cake(n)

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Documentary following zookeeper Giles Clark as he hand-rears Sumatran tiger cubs Spot and Stripe, which were born at the Australia Zoo in Queensland and are one of the world's most critically endangered species. To help give them the best start and ensure their survival, Giles takes them home to live with his boisterous family and gives them round-the-clock care for the first four months of their lives. The film gives an intimate look into the crucial early development of the cubs as they struggle through their first crucial weeks, while Giles embarks upon the toughest parenting job of his life and tries to save the species from the brink of extinction in the wild. Andrew Lincoln narrates(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Stephanie Connell and Charlie Ross journey from Dunstable to the Cotswolds, where Charlie comes across an ancient naval relic, but Stephanie finds something even rustier(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team from the world of arts and entertainment comprising actor Simon Greenall, singer Toyah Willcox, arts correspondent Nick Higham, award-winning saxophonist YolanDa Brown and Proms presenter Katie Derham set out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal, before visiting a cinema dating from 1912 in Clevedon(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Dominic Chinea undertakes the challenging restoration of a Carrom board, a traditional Asian board game, while Steven Kember teams up with silversmith Brenton West to repair a precious silver menorah. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher restores a small wooden duck once used in magic tricks, and bookbinder Christopher Shaw repairs a pair of photo albums charting Captain Scott's Antarctic expedition(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The contestants are split into pairs and challenged to transform shop interiors, revamping a series of independent stores on a high street in Nottingham in just two days. In a vintage store, the designers go right back to basics, stripping the very high walls and ceiling, while frustrations mount in a women's clothing store as last-minute changes are made to the design plans. With the shop makeovers complete, Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Mary Portas decide who has impressed, and who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Cannabis: Miracle Medicine or Dangerous Drug?
programme.desc: Horizon investigates the very latest medical and scientific research into the effects of cannabis on the brain and the body, with the medicinal form of the drug now legal. A&E doctor Javid Abdelmoneim wants to find out whether it will help or harm patients. He meets the young epilepsy patient responsible for changing the law around medicinal cannabis in the UK, and sees the remarkable effect it has on his condition. He visits a medicinal cannabis farm in Denmark to learn how a company known for growing the recreational drug are now producing medicinal cannabis to be exported all over Europe. He also travels to Israel, hoping to find out why they have been using cannabis as a medicine for more than 20 years, and meets scientists studying the safety and effectiveness of cannabis in treating pain(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: After the electricity goes out in Sam's home, Sunny's ex-husband Jeff suggests that she get a generator in case the power fails again. He drives her to the DIY store to buy one, and she ends up running into Robin and his daughter. On the way home, Jeff tries to kiss Sam, but she rejects him. Comedy-drama, created by Pamela Adlon, starring Olivia Edward, Hannah Alligood, Mikey Madison and Pamela Adlon(n)

credits.actor: Olivia Edward, Hannah Alligood, Mikey Madison, Pamela Adlon

programme.title: When Bridges Collapse: The Genoa Disaster
programme.desc: On August 14, 2018, the Ponte Morandi in Genoa collapsed, sending 27 vehicles plummeting to the valley below with the loss of 43 lives. programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel around Asia exploring the roots of some of their favourite dishes from the continent. They begin in Hong Kong, where they learn about the versatility of the wok in Cantonese cuisine at a street-food stall, while breakfast reveals a surprising legacy of British rule, before the duo are sent on their way with a traditional Chinese good luck ceremony(n)

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Spot and Stripe are nine weeks old and are becoming quite a handful as they explore the Clarks' home in suburban Queensland. Giles feeds them meat, which brings out some very adult tiger behaviour, and encourages their development by allowing them outside for the first time. The zookeeper also visits the rainforest of Sumatra - the home of the tigers in the wild - to see first-hand the poaching and persecution the big cats are subjected to in their natural habitat(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: Belgium v England (Kick-off 6.30pm). All the action from the friendly at Den Dreef Stadium in Leuven. England finished fourth in the recent World Cup and now turn their attention to Euro 2021, which they qualify for as hosts. Their opponents tonight did not qualify for the World Cup, but are looking to reach back-to-back European Championships and are currently on a good run of form, with their only defeat in the last 12 months coming in a friendly against world champions USA(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels between Taunton and Newton Abbot. Along the way he joins in with a fertility dance in Stogumber, visits a celebrated garden at Hestercombe to discover the fruits of an unusual partnership, and enjoys a trip aboard the longest heritage line in England(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: Six years ago, a man largely unknown in the West was given one of the most important jobs in the world. China's new President, Xi Jinping, became the leader of a rising superpower, commander of a formidable military and the head of an economy that is crucial to people's lives around the world. This documentary tells the story of his controversial six-year rule, and how he set about transforming China into a tightly controlled state where loyalty to the Communist Party appears to be valued above everything else(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The contestants are split into pairs and challenged to transform shop interiors, revamping a series of independent stores on a high street in Nottingham in just two days. In a vintage store, the designers go right back to basics, stripping the very high walls and ceiling, while frustrations mount in a women's clothing store as last-minute changes are made to the design plans. With the shop makeovers complete, Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Mary Portas decide who has impressed, and who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian travels to Mongolia, the most sparsely populated country on Earth. With the aid of a local TV host, he spends a week exploring the vast nation. He tries his hand at traditional wrestling and shamanic rituals, encounters an enormous statue of Genghis Khan, takes part in a hunting trip with eagles, and visits an energy centre in the middle of the desert. He also discovers how relationships are formed among nomadic societies, where people often live more than 100 miles away from anyone else(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams look for items at the Royal Highland Centre in Ingliston, Edinburgh, with help from experts Roo Irvine and David Harper. Natasha Raskin Sharp keeps an eye on proceedings and also finds a very special portrait(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In Cambridge, a £50,000 ethical loan helps turn an experiment into an enterprise for Kath, who uses natural beeswax to produce an alternative to cling film and plastic wrapping(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don makes a start on creating a wildflower patch. Adam Frost visits Sledmere Gardens in East Yorkshire where the borders have been planted with specific colour schemes in mind. The programme also catches up with a family in the Vale of Glamorgan whose members pack their small garden with colourful bedding plants for maximum impact(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares a selection of stress-free recipes designed to help save time and calm hectic lives, beginning with dishes for when people are in a rush. She begins by demonstrating a trick for turning pancakes into a raspberry and peanut butter tray bake, while an omelette wrap filled with sun-dried tomato paste, olives and mushrooms is prepared in under 10 minutes. Nadiya also joins a shift at the largest mushroom producer in the country to find out what it takes to meet the demand for this fast, flexible and flavour-packed ingredient(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Miranda Krestovnikoff and Simon King join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: House-proud couples from Buxton and Herefordshire swap homes to try out a new way of life, and see whether they enjoy the experience(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and the team resort to door knocking in their search for Dorothy Caines' heirs. Edward Burridge's history reveals a relative loved by celebrities(n)

programme.title: Emergency Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: Two British police officers hunt for criminals on the streets of Australia, and discover one of their suspects is another Brit. Plus, freak weather brings some bizarre casualties for an emergency vet from Bristol(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how certain British ingredients make traditional combinations, preparing a pork and apple dish and a pear and Stilton tart. They also find out how Yorkshire pudding was made in the 1700s, and discover it was not originally served with beef(n)

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: Spot and Stripe are outgrowing Giles Clark's home and developing adult behaviour, so the time comes for them to be reintegrated with the rest of the tigers at Australia Zoo. Their development is far from over, however, as they have their first swimming lesson and meet the adults they are going to share their lives with. But all is not well with Spot, who picks up a life-threatening eye condition(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Charlie Ross browse for items of interest - and potential value - around Leicestershire, Northamptonshire and Rutland. Veteran auctioneer Charlie and dealer and Road Trip debutant Stephanie each hope to win out at the thrilling final auction in Market Harborough(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: TV doctors David Bull, Ellie Cannon, Linda Papadopoulos, Ranj Singh and Javid Abdelmoneim take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the trail of an historic achievement made at the dawn of the Edwardian era, Michael Portillo investigates the first radio signal to be sent across the Atlantic. Then, in Plymouth, he uncovers what happened to surviving crew members of the most famous ocean liner in history, the Titanic. Later, at Fowey, the former politician rediscovers a lost literary figure known as Q, who immortalised the town in his novels(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric and Bobby conclude their journey around the United Kingdom by heading to Scotland, where they visit the Edinburgh birthplace of 19th-century scientist James Clerk Maxwell, whose pioneering work includes the 1865 theory of electromagnetism. The duo then analyse the science behind the deep-fried Mars Bar before heading to Glasgow explore the Hunterian Museum's collection of antiquarian experimental instruments and delve into the personal correspondence of mathematical physicist and engineer Lord Kelvin(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair, facing questions on specialised subjects including national flags, the life of Napoleon Bonaparte, CS Lewis's The Chronicles of Narnia and the life and career of tennis player Andre Agassi. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don adds some ornamental grasses to his borders for late summer impact and gives advice on how to look after pumpkins for a bountiful autumn harvest. Adam Frost discovers a colourful garden in Gloucestershire where hardy, tender and tropical plants jostle for space in both borders and containers, while Frances Tophill visits an allotment in north London where the main harvest is flowers for floral displays(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.sub-title: All I Care About Is Love
programme.desc: The stress begins to get to Bob as he finishes the editing of Lenny, which has considerably overrun. Gwen prepares for the first day of the rehearsal for Chicago, where she has a cast-iron contract with the producers, with approval over almost every aspect of the production. As rehearsals begin Bob experiences chest pains and visits a hospital emergency room. Gwen arranges with a Broadway-loving doctor that Bob is given his own room, and assures him that Chicago won't proceed until he is better. True-life drama, starring Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended edition of the comedy quiz show, posing more perplexing questions to a selection of celebrity guests. As ever, the panellists will not necessarily be expected to deliver the correct answers, and Sandi will be handing out points to those who provide the 'most interesting' guesses. Challengers joining regular quizzer Alan Davies this week are Bill Bailey, Phill Jupitus and Claudia Winkleman(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Mark Urban presents analysis of the day's events(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: A Black and White Killing: The Case That Shook America
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mobeen Azhar investigates the murder of Larnell Bruce by a member of a white supremacist gang in Portland, and assesses the degree to which race played a role in the choice of victim. The investigation delves into the history of the seemingly liberal city, and uncovers a dark underbelly of racial tensions in the state of Oregon. The programme also examines the events of the trial, which were marked by clashes between anti-fascist groups and white supremacists on the streets. Featuring interviews with members of the militant groups and expert witnesses called on to testify(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: History of the Future: Cars
programme.desc: Phill Jupitus examines how cars of the future were predicted to look in the 1950s and 1960s. In his quest to trace the dream car of his childhood, he visits the Henry Ford Museum and the GM Heritage Centre in Detroit, as well as the Science Museum in London, with contributions from Jonathan Glancy and Clive Sinclair(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the importance of Gaelic to the country's past and looks at the impact of a feud between two clans on the Highlands and Lowlands. He examines how the policies of the Stewarts in the 15th century led to the Macdonalds being perceived as rebels and outsiders, and to Scots becoming the dominant language(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Landfill: A Rubbish History
programme.desc: In a unique science experiment, Dr George McGavin and Dr Zoe Laughlin are given unprecedented access to one of the UK's largest landfill sites to investigate the history of rubbish(n)

programme.title: Susan Calman's Fringe Benefits
programme.desc: The host catches up with one-time Blue Peter presenter Janet Ellis, who has been appearing at the book festival. She also meets acting legends Arabella Weir and Cora Bissett, and comedians Nish Kumar and Mark Nelson(n)

programme.title: The Friday Documentary
programme.desc: In a documentary first aired in 1989, Mini enthusiasts around the world celebrate the famous small car's 30th birthday. Contributors, including comedian Spike Milligan and politician Tony Benn, explain the little car's unique character(n)

programme.title: Jane Austen: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley embarks on a road trip across England to take in some of the places Jane Austen lived in and visited during her lifetime. She aims to demonstrate how Jane's personal experiences influenced the central themes of home and property in her novels, and makes use of detective-work to recreate some of the most important locations that no longer stand. She begins in Stoneleigh Abbey, Warwickshire, retracing a journey that marked one of the many times the Austens veered wildly between wealth and genteel poverty - mirroring the Dashwoods of Sense and Sensibility. Lucy also travels from Jane's birthplace in Steventon, Hampshire, to her brother's grand estate at Godmersham Park - the inspiration for Darcy's Pemberley in Pride and Prejudice, along with Lyme Regis, Bath and Southampton(n)

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the role of artists and architects in the development of American cities and culture, as the country sought to step out of Europe's shadow. He travels to Manhattan, Chicago and Massachusetts to examine the work of Norman Rockwell, John Sloan, George Bellows and Louis Sullivan, before charting the links between the Great Depression of the 1920s and 30s and the rise of abstract expressionism. He also visits Jackson Pollock's studio in Long Island and Mark Rothko's Chapel in Houston, Texas(n)

programme.title: Sings Bacharach and David!
programme.desc: Archive performances of songs penned by Burt Bacharach and Hal David, featuring artists including Aretha Franklin, Dusty Springfield, the Carpenters, Sandie Shaw and the Stranglers. The programme also features Bacharach himself performing alongside Rufus Wainwright on Later with Jools Holland(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the importance of Gaelic to the country's past and looks at the impact of a feud between two clans on the Highlands and Lowlands. He examines how the policies of the Stewarts in the 15th century led to the Macdonalds being perceived as rebels and outsiders, and to Scots becoming the dominant language(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Mark Goodier introduce the edition first aired on May 26, featuring music by Debbie Gibson, LA Mix, Ofra Haza, Climie Fisher, Aswad, the Style Council, Hothouse Flowers, Mica Paris, Scritti Politti, Wet Wet Wet and Heart(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Two aspiring air traffic controllers begin the rigorous three-year training course, while an amateur pilot prepares for her first solo flight at Elstree Airfield, London. A resident of a once-quiet village in Kent leads a campaign to stop the narrowing of flight paths and increased noise overhead(n)

programme.title: World War Speed: The Drugs that Won WWII
programme.desc: Historian James Holland embarks on a quest to dig deeper and unearth the truth behind the role drugs such as methamphetamine played in the Second World War(n)

programme.title: WWII: Operation Crossbow
programme.desc: The little-known story of Spitfire pilots and Allied technicians whose work helped to thwart the Nazis during the Second World War. The programme reveals how stereoscopic 3D photographs were used to help interpreters map every contour of the enemy's territory, and uses personal testimony, computer-generated imagery and original wartime pictures to detail how the initiative was able to uncover some of Hitler's most dangerous secrets. Narrated by Samantha Bond(n)

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: The historian reveals hows Egypt's enemies exploited a country weakened by internal strife, travelling south to Sudan to examine the story of the forgotten Nubian kings, who ruled Egypt from their southern homeland for a century, even building their own pyramids to bury their monarchs. She then explores how the next group of invaders the Saites took the tradition of mummification to new extremes by preserving millions of animals, before Alexander the Great came to Egypt's rescue and founded the greatest city on Earth(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Mark Goodier introduce the edition first aired on May 26, featuring music by Debbie Gibson, LA Mix, Ofra Haza, Climie Fisher, Aswad, the Style Council, Hothouse Flowers, Mica Paris, Scritti Politti, Wet Wet Wet and Heart(n)

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his journey through American art by examining how it reflected and challenged the country's post-war culture. He begins in the purpose-built suburb of Levittown, Pennsylvania, before travelling to New York to examine works by Jasper Johns and Andy Warhol, then revealing how the otherworldly qualities of Los Angeles inspired painter Ed Ruscha. The presenter also discusses his love for the work of Philip Guston, meets Warhol's former lover Billy Name and artists Jeff Koons and James Rosenquist, and assesses artistic responses to the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Two aspiring air traffic controllers begin the rigorous three-year training course, while an amateur pilot prepares for her first solo flight at Elstree Airfield, London. programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.sub-title: BBC Proms: Strauss, Sibelius and Prokofiev
programme.desc: Katie Derham presents flavours of Finland, Russia and Germany from three 20th-century favourites. Yannick Nézet-Séguin and the Bavarian Radio Symphony Orchestra perform Sibelius's turbulent first Symphony, Prokofiev's second Violin Concerto - performed by Gil Shaham - and the orchestral suite from Richard Strauss's blockbuster opera, Der Rosenkavalier(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019

programme.desc: Clara Amfo introduces a prom celebrating jazz and blues legend Nina Simone. The Metropole Orkest is led by Jules Buckley, joined by Ledisi, Lisa Fischer and other singers in this tribute to the acclaimed singer, songwriter and political activist. The titles include standards such as Feeling Good, My Baby Just Cares for Me and I Put a Spell on You(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Traditionally Martha and Jas have had a joint birthday party, with their birthdays falling close together. Martha and Mrs Fitzgerald have up until now led the way in terms of the choice of party theme - characteristically formal, complete with lobster and butler - and not much fun. When Jas and her dad suggest 'shaking it up' a bit this year and introducing a Mexican theme, it leads first of all to the parents falling out and then to Martha and Jas themselves. It's the battle of the lobster vs the Mexican donkey piñata called Percy(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: The class learns about the Women's Movement and feminism, and the girls put what their lessons into action as they protest against needless airbrushing of year-book photos(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Jas decides to follow in her dad's footsteps and try out for the junior hockey team. Mr Salford was a legendary hockey goalie in his time at the school. When the try-outs are cancelled due to the coach not being available, Mr Salford steps in and offers his services as coach. Mrs Griggs is horrified - she could do without the chaos he inevitably brings with him - but he won't take no for an answer. Jas and her father are really superstitious and when things start going wrong Jas thinks she's jinxed, and then that she's passed on the jinx to her dad. When she's not picked as goalie for the team Jas is actually relieved - it must mean she's still jinxed and hasn't passed on the jinx to her dad. Eventually Jas does need to play in goal, but when she saves a crucial penalty she and her dad are delighted to realise they are not jinxed after all, just accident-prone! Meanwhile, Martha & Ollie get paranoid that Cassie & Maxwell are usurping them. They've taken over their favourite nook in the(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Mrs Griggs decides that a 'social skills' class could be beneficial for certain pupils - including Martha, Rufus, Cassie and Maxwell. When Mrs Griggs tells Martha a winning personality as well as academic achievement is vital in order to get into a top university, Martha panics and is determined to succeed in the social skills class and prove she does indeed have a winning personality. Mrs Griggs volunteers a reluctant Mr Malone to take the classes and hands him a book written by her beloved 'Guru Holeman' called Being Whole as a manual to help him. When Lily is mistakenly led to believe by Jas that Matt is emigrating to Australia she is distraught that he hasn't even told her he's leaving. She becomes convinced it's because she's too easy-going that he hasn't even bothered to tell her. She begs Mrs Griggs to let her go to social skills class, too, so she can learn to be less easy-going. Ollie is then sent there for being too pompous and Jas for being too exuberant. Worn down, eventual(n)

His mother's Irish parents died when she was very young, so Paul sets out to piece together the story of his grandfather's involvement in key events leading up to Irish independence in the 1920s, and learns that the true circumstances of his death were very different from the story that had been handed down. programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould examine a still life that may have been painted by prolific artist William Nicholson, but which has been rejected by leading experts on his work. They discover a useful source of evidence in Nicholson's own paint box, but as they delve into the painting's history, they discover it may have been connected to one of the 20th century's greatest art crimes. Fiona meets a reformed forger to discover if he ever faked a Nicholson while Philip takes the painting to Canada to compare it to another of the artist's works(n)

programme.title: Richard and Jaco Take on the World
programme.desc: A personal exploration of autism with actor Richard Mylan and his autistic son Jaco. The pair journey from Wales to America and back again, exploring what it means to be autistic and finding out how places from a local barbershop to a national stadium can become more inclusive for autistic people. They visit Sesame Place theme park in Pennsylvania, where Jaco has a chance to meet Julia, Sesame Street's first character with autism(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is house hunting with a couple who have a budget of £650,000 and want to quit the city to find a house with great entertaining potential in the Cheshire countryside. Sonali also delves into the region's manufacturing heritage at one of the country's best preserved textile mills from the Industrial Revolution(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a successful time at Bredbury recycling centre, Sarah Moore hopes to generate some profit from two hidden gems. It's up to Josh and Oli to reimagine some discarded logs, while Sarah has to come up with a great idea to transform some old pipes into fabulous and desirable centerpieces(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the murder of a man in 1930s Lincolnshire. It was discovered that Arthur Major died as a result of strychnine poisoning and 42-year-old Ethel Major was arrested and charged with wilful murder. She protested her innocence and at trial was defended by a famous barrister, Norman Birkett KC, but the jury took just one hour to declare her guilty(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Anita Manning are at Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where they assess items including a selection of golf memorabilia and a trio of Moorcroft ceramics(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw and Emma Willis chat to celebrity guests and introduce stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In mid-Wales, Kate and sheepdog Teg have a taste of stone-age life mucking in with a farmer and an archaeologist who are building a medieval-type settlement. Next, they explore the potential death traps of historical legacies at an old lead mine, where the South and Mid Wales Cave Rescue Team practise their skills using Teg as the damsel in distress. Kate also learns about the plight of Welsh mountain ponies, whose numbers have dropped dramatically, and discovers whether her legs are built for mountain biking(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes the final preparations to leave Walford and as she covers her tracks at work, her behaviour makes Sharon suspicious. After one too many drinks, Mick takes Linda home and Stuart reassures him regarding Callum's whereabouts. As Robbie hopelessly flirts with Ash, Iqra kisses her in front of everyone(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: The second part of a two-legged edition of the quiz, which sees former England striker Darren Bent, 1993 and 1999 world champion 110m hurdler Colin Jackson, world Taekwondo title-winner Bianca Walkden and Olympic heptathlon medallist Kelly Sotherton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: With her annoying sister Joan safely packed off home, Edith is thrilled when Phil announces that Jack Dugdale has made an offer on his house and he has accepted. Now they can get on and plan their future together, starting by setting a date for their wedding. However, it transpires that romance is in the air for more than just the one couple. Roger, Georgie and Bob attend a children's party, but are soon sent packing, while the family's portrayal in Wendy's book continues to cause angst. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Denise Lewis, Richard Osman, Robert Rinder and Katherine Ryan to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out to discover the truth about work. It's a story of bouncers, bin men and female wrestlers, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: The King's Speech
programme.desc: The younger son of George V struggles to cope with an uncontrollable stammer, prompting his wife to enlist the aid of an eccentric Australian speech therapist. The support and friendship of the doctor prove invaluable when a crisis places the repressed prince on the throne, just as the outbreak of the Second World War leaves the country in need of a strong king. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Geoffrey Rush, Helena Bonham Carter and Guy Pearce(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Stratford-upon-Avon, Simon and Steve want a garden that mixes modern looks with heritage touches and dramatic twists. The team sets about devising something that's equal part both stylishly modern and with hints of Tudor heritage(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Police from the UK and Uganda trace a woman who fled Britain after killing her daughter, and officers in London are on the hunt for a man wanted in Italy for committing rape and serious assault. Meanwhile in Yorkshire, police search for a criminal who used a light aircraft to smuggle cocaine(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton investigates his family history. His mother's Irish parents died when she was very young, so Paul sets out to piece together the story of his grandfather's involvement in key events leading up to Irish independence in the 1920s, and learns that the true circumstances of his death were very different from the story that had been handed down. Elsewhere in London, Paul traces a great, great grandmother on his father's side of the family who was sent to prison for her part in an assault(n)

programme.title: Heartbreak Holiday
programme.desc: Documentary following 10 strangers united by heartbreak as they head off for the summer of a lifetime around the Greek islands. In the final episode, emotions run high as the life-changing holiday comes to an end. The girls decide to confront Luke, and Archie and Melissa challenge themselves at sea(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Cherry Healey and a team of independent scientists conduct an experiment that puts everyday cosmetic and beauty products to the test. Cherry discovers that products such a moisturisers may not have the benefits we once thought, and reveals an easy fix for cellulite that actually works. The programme also looks at the psychology behind why we buy certain cosmetic and beauty products(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: From Syria to Scotland - Our Lives
programme.desc: The story of Syrian refugee families who have made their homes on the island of Bute in the Firth of Clyde, finding a place of safety after the horrors of the Syrian war they have escaped. Mounzer runs the Orient Salon, named after the busy barber shop he ran in his native Damascus, while next door Rahaf and her husband Fadel run a restaurant and takeaway bringing a taste of Syria to Bute. Morven Christie narrates(n)

programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms and then sells three things about to be dumped at a tip in Witley, Surrey, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items. Items include some discarded oak and an old armchair. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick feels the pressure to ensure everything is perfect on the day of Whitney and Callum's hen and stag parties. When Stuart learns Ben is coming, his mood shifts and he sneaks off. Tiffany's interest is piqued when Sonia admits she is planning a big surprise, while Jack weighs up the idea of returning to the police force after speaking to Muski(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Tommy and Adie deal with an unusual call out to a sheep farm in Wales, while Rob and Gerald turn detective as they search for a pub landlord who has not paid for his stock. Emotions run high when the sheriffs visit an Italian car mechanic who owes money in a dispute over a van repair(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould examine a still life that may have been painted by prolific artist William Nicholson, but which has been rejected by leading experts on his work. They discover a useful source of evidence in Nicholson's own paint box, but as they delve into the painting's history, they discover it may have been connected to one of the 20th century's greatest art crimes. Fiona meets a reformed forger to discover if he ever faked a Nicholson while Philip takes the painting to Canada to compare it to another of the artist's works(n)

programme.title: Getaways
programme.desc: Joe Lindsay and Vogue Williams take advantage of the new direct air route to the former British colony of Hong Kong in China. With more than seven million residents and more skyscrapers than any other city in the world, it's a mix of East and West, new and ancient. Plus, Tommy Bowe explores Carlingford, Co Louth(n)

programme.title: High Life
programme.desc: Genevieve, a very sensible, creative and overachieving 17-year-old student in a respectable, middle-class family, seems to be having the perfect ride, until her sanity spectacularly unravels in her first manic episode of bipolar disorder(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian Paul Merton investigates his family history. His mother's Irish parents died when she was very young, so Paul sets out to piece together the story of his grandfather's involvement in key events leading up to Irish independence in the 1920s, and learns that the true circumstances of his death were very different from the story that had been handed down. Elsewhere in London, Paul traces a great, great grandmother on his father's side of the family who was sent to prison for her part in an assault(n)

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Steph McGovern present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Summer Diaries
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor discovers how a secret wartime mission behind enemy lines brought one of our favourite seasonings, garlic, to the Isle of Wight. Keeley Donovan learns how a group of dedicated volunteers came to the dramatic rescue of a stranded sunburnt dolphin that was clinging to life(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell revisits a bungalow in Tipton, Dion Dublin meets father-and-daughter developers in Burnley and Martin Roberts examines the potential of three flats in Lewisham, south-east London(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In St Ives, 24-year-old Tassy turns to the internet to crowd-fund a surf school, hoping to help local girls achieve their potential and increase their self-confidence(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to Petworth in West Sussex to meet a group of residents entering Bloom for the first time who are hoping to get a disconnected community working together. These Bloomers want to engage the whole area, from the historic town centre to the modern housing estates on the outskirts, in an effort to bring the community spirit back to their neighbourhood(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is house hunting with a couple who have a budget of £650,000 and want to quit the city to find a house with great entertaining potential in the Cheshire countryside. Sonali also delves into the region's manufacturing heritage at one of the country's best preserved textile mills from the Industrial Revolution(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: After a successful time at Bredbury recycling centre, Sarah Moore hopes to generate some profit from two hidden gems. It's up to Josh and Oli to reimagine some discarded logs, while Sarah has to come up with a great idea to transform some old pipes into fabulous and desirable centerpieces(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the murder of a man in 1930s Lincolnshire. It was discovered that Arthur Major died as a result of strychnine poisoning and 42-year-old Ethel Major was arrested and charged with wilful murder. She protested her innocence and at trial was defended by a famous barrister, Norman Birkett KC, but the jury took just one hour to declare her guilty(n)

programme.title: Flog It!

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist Emma and Steve, whose front garden in Hoveringham overlooking the River Trent, feels very much 'on display' to passers-by. The couple want greater want privacy without losing the precious river view and also wish to utilise a half-boat that they own in the design. Both Charlie and the siblings propose a similar clever idea to solve the privacy problem, a sunken courtyard garden. This only makes it harder for Emma and Steve to decide which design to pick(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel makes the final preparations to leave Walford and as she covers her tracks at work, her behaviour makes Sharon suspicious. After one too many drinks, Mick takes Linda home and Stuart reassures him regarding Callum's whereabouts. As Robbie hopelessly flirts with Ash, Iqra kisses her in front of everyone(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: The second part of a two-legged edition of the quiz, which sees former England striker Darren Bent, 1993 and 1999 world champion 110m hurdler Colin Jackson, world Taekwondo title-winner Bianca Walkden and Olympic heptathlon medallist Kelly Sotherton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: With her annoying sister Joan safely packed off home, Edith is thrilled when Phil announces that Jack Dugdale has made an offer on his house and he has accepted. Now they can get on and plan their future together, starting by setting a date for their wedding. However, it transpires that romance is in the air for more than just the one couple. Roger, Georgie and Bob attend a children's party, but are soon sent packing, while the family's portrayal in Wendy's book continues to cause angst. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins and John Cleese(n)

programme.title: The King's Speech
programme.desc: The younger son of George V struggles to cope with an uncontrollable stammer, prompting his wife to enlist the aid of an eccentric Australian speech therapist. The support and friendship of the doctor prove invaluable when a crisis places the repressed prince on the throne, just as the outbreak of the Second World War leaves the country in need of a strong king. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Colin Firth, Geoffrey Rush, Helena Bonham Carter and Guy Pearce(n)

programme.title: Cuimhneachan (Remembrance)
programme.desc: The legacy of the Great War through the eyes of the bards who were writing in the years after the conflict. It was thought it would be 'the war to end all wars', but it wasn't and that realisation led many poets such as Murdo MacFarlane to question the effectiveness of war in general. However, at the same time it was realised that the sacrifice of those who went into battle should never be forgotten. Featuring specially recorded performances by Gaelic singers Art Cormack, Linda MacLeod, Murdo MacDonald and Robert Robertson(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: In the Cairngorm mountains in Scotland, a group of handlers and their search and rescue dogs undergo final training in the snowy peaks. Every search and rescue dog will train for up to two years before it is deemed ready to act within mountain rescue teams looking for lost and injured walkers. In the Galtee Mountains of Tipperary, Search and Rescue dog Ziggy is searching for an injured walker somewhere near Galtee mountain, while in the Peak District the Kinder Mountain Rescue Team attends a walker in severe pain with a broken ankle(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police and a reporter turn up at the school as Tyrone pleads with Evelyn to see sense, and Robert says he has CCTV of Jed trying to blackmail him. Realising Tim is in the dark, Yasmeen urges Geoff to tell him about his illness(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Cole's former mentor from his CIA years Tom Barnes appears with some alarming news, as he and Murtaugh set about investigating the robbery of safe deposit boxes, whose suspects seem connected to his past. Meanwhile, Murtaugh encounters his wife's new client Louie Gutierrez. 'The Gute', as she is colloquially known, immediately proves to be a strong adversary, causing upset with every move she makes, prompting him to enlist Bailey's help to investigate her. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott, with Chandler Kinney(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar return as detectives Cassie and Sunny, who find themselves thrown into another historic murder case with the discover of a body. This time it's the perfectly preserved remains of a middle-aged man found in a suitcase in the silt of a London river. As the detectives begin the complicated task of trying to identify the victim, four unconnected people go about their business - Brighton-based lawyer Colin Osborne, paediatric nurse Marion Kelsey, teacher Sara Mahmoud and respected police officer Tessa Nixon - unaware they are about to be dragged into the investigation. Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar star, with Mark Bonnar, Lorraine Ashbourne, Charlie Condou and Wendy Craig(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: With their victim identified, Cassie and Sunny begin to find out a bit more about David Walker by talking to his ex-wife, DI Tessa Nixon, who reveals he suffered from depression. A search through the original investigation files and some numbers from David's pager help build a picture of him - and also lead to a few potential suspects. However, his son Jason struggles to deal with the news that someone murdered his dad. Whodunit, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Lorraine Ashbourne, Mark Bonnar, Douglas Hodge, Nigel Lindsay and Will Brown(n)

programme.title: Vera

Crime drama based on Ann Cleeves' series of novels, starring Brenda Blethyn(n)

programme.title: Lewis

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Secrets could be exposed as Rosamund arrives at Downton following Edith's shock departure. Rose is delighted to introduce Atticus and his parents to the Abbey, while Anna and Bates start thinking about a fresh start, now that the worst of their problems are seemingly behind them. Molesley is concerned to see Daisy becoming disillusioned with her studies, and Isobel makes a happy announcement, but there may be trouble on the horizon. Violet has her hands full with her warring servants, while Mary and Blake seize an opportunity to send Gillingham a strong message(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War

Police drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Brendan delights in telling Reg that one of the managers must go, Des accuses Doug of messing up his life, and Raquel's romantic afternoon gets spoilt(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

Nostalgic drama, starring Philip Franks, Jason Durr and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Journalist Jim Nash is murdered in the swamps of New Orleans, where voodoo is still a force to be reckoned with. Jessica risks its horrors and rituals to unmask the culprit. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey organises an exhibition as the bus company celebrates its half-centenary. Stan reminisces, and on falling asleep finds himself at the wheel of a bus from the turn of the 20th century. Comedy. starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is offered a job in a car factory in the midlands and decides the best way to quit his job at the bus depot is by getting himself sacked in order to earn an extra week's wages. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: A valuable heirloom brought back from the China Sea could change the Ropers' fortunes - but not if Mildred's sister gets it first. Starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A barmaid's murder leads Lewis (Kevin Whately) and Hathaway (Laurence Fox) to a respected professor, who admits he once had an affair with the woman. However, they discover the killing was copied straight from the pages of a popular fantasy novel written by an Oxford tutor, suggesting it may be the work of a crazed fan - or something even more sinister. Guest starring Art Malik, Tom Mison and James Fox, father of Laurence(n)

credits.actor: Art Malik, Tom Mison, James Fox, father of Laurence

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New sergeant Dennis Merton arrives at Ashfordly police station and soon finds himself embroiled in a CID stakeout - but Ventress's shady past threatens the entire operation. Meanwhile, all efforts to pummel David into the peak of physical fitness look set to come to nothing, until a chance encounter knocks Vernon for six. Nostalgic drama, starring William Simons and Duncan Bell(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth is suspicious when Eric says he cannot make it to the wedding rehearsal, his stag do does not go to plan, and Kathy is upset about Chris's ultimatum(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth panics on the morning of her wedding, while Eric is sound asleep in the Woolpack cellar. Michael is shocked to hear that Steve has been arrested for the burglary(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg informs Curly he has put in for redundancy, Audrey advises Alf they should accept Holdsworth's offer, and Ivy tells Don her plans for the shop(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel prepares a special meal for Wayne, Curly breaks the news to Deirdre that Reg has bought the shop, and Bet is shocked to hear Liz has been given the brewery's top pub(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A student production of The Merchant of Venice is halted when the actor playing Shylock is murdered during the performance. Lewis and Hathaway are struck by the lack of grief among the cast and crew - especially the ambitious director, who wastes no time recasting the role. The detectives discover the victim had an expensive lifestyle and not only owed money to his fellow students, but was also suspected of stealing from them. However, the investigation has barely got under way before the killer strikes again. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Lady Cora enjoys the attention Simon Bricker gives her, but is he about to cross a line - and does she want him to? Cora's eldest child, Mary, is also enjoying male company, this time that of Charles Blake, who's about to do her a big favour. Meanwhile, Rose meets a dashing stranger, Branson makes up his mind about Sarah Bunting and Anna is disturbed by a visit from Inspector Vyner(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Two elderly people, both under the care of the same visiting nurse, die almost simultaneously and Jessica is asked to get to the bottom of the mystery. Angela Lansbury stars, with William Windom and Audra Lindley(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris, Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar return as detectives Cassie and Sunny, who find themselves thrown into another historic murder case with the discover of a body. This time it's the perfectly preserved remains of a middle-aged man found in a suitcase in the silt of a London river. As the detectives begin the complicated task of trying to identify the victim, four unconnected people go about their business - Brighton-based lawyer Colin Osborne, paediatric nurse Marion Kelsey, teacher Sara Mahmoud and respected police officer Tessa Nixon - unaware they are about to be dragged into the investigation. Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar star, with Mark Bonnar, Lorraine Ashbourne, Charlie Condou and Wendy Craig(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: With their victim identified, Cassie and Sunny begin to find out a bit more about David Walker by talking to his ex-wife, DI Tessa Nixon, who reveals he suffered from depression. A search through the original investigation files and some numbers from David's pager help build a picture of him - and also lead to a few potential suspects. However, his son Jason struggles to deal with the news that someone murdered his dad. Whodunit, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Lorraine Ashbourne, Mark Bonnar, Douglas Hodge, Nigel Lindsay and Will Brown(n)

credits.actor: Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Lorraine Ashbourne, Mark Bonnar, Douglas Hodge, Nigel Lindsay, Will Brown

programme.desc: A respected gynaecologist is accused of sexually assaulting a patient during a routine examination, but Brooks, Devlin and Chandler struggle to find any firm evidence and the team becomes divided. Crown Prosecutor Alesha Phillips refuses to accept defeat and takes on formidable defence barrister Phyllis Gladstone. Crime drama, guest starring Derek Riddell and Lesley Manville, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter and Freema Agyeman(n)

credits.actor: Derek Riddell, Lesley Manville, Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Harriet Walter, Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A tragic communication error between officers leaves gay police constable Nick Bentley caught in the crossfire of armed drug dealers, with no back-up. Brooks and Devlin follow a lead to a small-time dealer who claims to have seen a second policeman standing nearby, doing nothing while his partner bled to death. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Harriet Walter(n)

programme.title: Vera

Crime drama based on Ann Cleeves' series of novels, starring Brenda Blethyn(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan is joined by an old friend - Turkish policeman Captain Shebbeq - to help him track down a gold-smuggling ring. Nadim Sawalha guest stars, with John Thaw(n)

programme.title: Cycling: Vuelta a Espana
programme.desc: Action from the fourth stage of the Grand Tour race, which featured a 175.5km course from Cullera to El Puig. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from David Millar, reports from Daniel Friebe and commentary by Ned Boulting and David Millar(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates the suspicious deaths of five company directors, who each received a card with the words 'The Scales of Justice' printed on it prior to their demise. However, he finds his inquiries frustrated by the remaining members of the board. Roger Moore stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Detective Crocker's loyalties are tested when an old friend is suspected of being involved with a protection racket. Guest starring Brad Dexter (The Magnificent Seven)(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A microchip found in a dead Navy officer's body sparks off another tricky investigation for Quincy, who struggles to make sense of events(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur gets his hands on the unreleased recording of a late, lamented pop star's final song, and decides to launch himself as a music-biz entrepreneur. Mel Smith guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to keep a surveillance on Cowley after evidence suggests he has stolen two canisters of a lethal nerve gas and begun negotiating with an unfriendly foreign power. programme.title: Tomorrow Never Dies
programme.desc: James Bond joins forces with a Chinese agent to foil a media mogul's scheme to start a war between their countries - a case complicated by the fact that an old flame is now married to his new enemy. Using an undetectable stealth ship, the press baron plans to launch an undetectable sneak attack on both nations' navies, knowing they will blame each other - giving him exclusive global media coverage of the resulting conflict. Spy adventure, starring Pierce Brosnan, Jonathan Pryce, Teri Hatcher and Michelle Yeoh(n)

credits.actor: Pierce Brosnan, Jonathan Pryce, Teri Hatcher, Michelle Yeoh

Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, James Caan, Vanessa L Williams and James Coburn(n)

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A dull American town in the middle of the desert is suddenly attacked by huge man-eating subterranean creatures. Luckily, two dimwitted but brave handymen step into the breach to lead a ragtag bunch of locals and a mismatched pair of survivalists in the battle against the gruesome worm-like monsters. Comedy horror, with Kevin Bacon, Fred Ward, Finn Carter, Michael Gross, Reba McEntire and Bobby Jacoby(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar agrees to act as go-between, taking ransom money to the kidnappers of the wayward daughter of a rich film producer. However, he has a trick up his sleeve - quite literally - in the shape of a camera disguised as a wrist-watch, so even though he is blindfolded, he can later retrace his steps. Roger Moore directs and stars(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Captain McNeil's wife is kidnapped by a drug dealer who demands the police destroy all evidence of his crimes. A letter she left for her husband may hold a clue to her whereabouts(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner performs an autopsy when a man demands answers over the death of his brother, who died following an operation by an eminent surgeon(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry intervenes in a tug-of-love dispute concerning an Anglo-Italian Mancini family, and finds himself unwittingly threatening the success of Arthur's latest money-making venture - a health club. Dennis Waterman and George Cole star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are pitted against KGB assassins when a CI5 operative is apparently raised from the dead. A proposed exchange finds Cowley at a deserted aerodrome, and a burst of gunfire apparently leaves his life on the line. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.title: Cycling: Vuelta a Espana

programme.title: The Sports Stars Fishing Championship
programme.desc: Action from Golden Valley Lakes in Hampshire, where 32 competitors competed over four hours, fishing in eight teams of four. Among the stars from the world of sport taking part with the aim of raising money for Parkinson's UK were ex-boxer George Groves and former footballers David Seaman and Jimmy Bullard(n)

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A dull American town in the middle of the desert is suddenly attacked by huge man-eating subterranean creatures. Luckily, two dimwitted but brave handymen step into the breach to lead a ragtag bunch of locals and a mismatched pair of survivalists in the battle against the gruesome worm-like monsters. Comedy horror, with Kevin Bacon, Fred Ward, Finn Carter, Michael Gross, Reba McEntire and Bobby Jacoby(n)

The film meets the roller coaster junkies keen to try the new ride; and with exclusive access to the theme park, follows the team at Alton Towers as they battle with the challenge of building a wooden structure that appears to burst into flames. programme.desc: Babak and Alison from Barnsley are planning a permanent move to Almeria in southern Spain. The couple want to head inland and focus their search around the host of pretty towns and villages this mountainous region has to offer. Danni Menzies has five options within their maximum budget of £145,000, but will any of them match the pair's expectations of a dream home?(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now it's down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michon's from disaster(n)

programme.title: Britain's Extreme Weather: Superstorms & Heatwaves
programme.desc: Morland Sanders looks at climate change and its link to severe weather events in the UK, which has seem tornadoes, torrential rain, lightning storms, flash floods and a record-breaking temperature of 38.7C in recent months. This programme features archive footage, personal stories and striking visuals of Britain's recent heatwave. He examines whether the nation's ageing infrastructure can cope with this ever more brutal onslaught, and what effect it will have on agriculture and the cost of living(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: To celebrate 20 years of Grand Designs, Kevin McCloud's reveals his top five properties from the show, revisiting the buildings and people who have most inspired him over the years. From concrete-vaulted glass-fronted pavilions and heroic restoration projects, to handcrafted hobbit houses made of hedgerow, Kevin reflects on what it takes to build a truly great Grand Designs project(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, Judith, a 57-year-old breast cancer survivor from Nuneaton, and Rigby, a 27-year-old hot tub installer from Chesterfield, take part(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Holiday Resort
programme.desc: Another look at life at an all-inclusive Holiday World Resort in Malaga, Spain, to see what Britons get up to while on holiday in the sun and what it takes to keep them happy. Liverpudlian friends Paddy, Steve and John take time out from their families to take advantage of the all-inclusive bar and the full range of the evening entertainment the resort has on offer(n)

programme.title: Victor Frankenstein
programme.desc: A sideshow performer is taken in by a scientist, who seeks his help in performing experiments to create life, and hopefully uncover the secrets of immortality. The plan goes dangerously out of control, and it falls to the devoted assistant to save his master from his dark obsessions and his monstrous creation. Gothic horror, starring James McAvoy and Daniel Radcliffe(n)

programme.title: The Sex Clinic
programme.desc: Khayda is worried that the spots on his bum are the first signs of something more serious and Emelio is concerned he might be having too much fun in the bedroom. Jacob wants a sex test as he never uses condoms, while Elle is desperate for tips on how to have a pain-free sex life(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Lucia and Matt, who were split over what to do with their four-bed detached home in Royal Wootton Bassett, Wiltshire. With four cats, two dogs and a yard full of hens, the house was at bursting point. Kirstie heads to the pair's new home in Llangammarch Wells, Powys, where huge changes have occurred, including the addition of a peacock to the household menagerie. Do Lucia and Matt feel they got the life they really wanted after all?(n)

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Phil and Christine push cake and engineering boundaries with a steampunk flying machine - with real smoke. Nastassja goes 'speaker crazy' for a DJ's 30th birthday cake, and the pressure is on Suzanne to please cake-loving clients with a dream wedding cake(n)

programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers
programme.desc: In Kent, bosses and brothers Charlie and Patrick at Nikolas Patrick consider doing deals on a £30,000 designer dress and a pair of very unusual antique 'rolling-eye' clocks. At Pickwick Pawnbrokers in London, boss Nathan hits the road to go and examine an unusual retro 'Bubbler' jukebox and a vintage 1950s coffee machine. A rare 260-year-old tortoiseshell and silver pocket watch may also be worth a small fortune(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The culinary contest arrives in Hull as five more diners hope to claim the grand prize of £1,000. The first to host a party this week is 29-year-old welfare team leader Jennie, who decides to honour her home city's fishing history with an under-the-sea themed dinner. However, her guests prove to be a lively bunch, twerking their way through dinner, and throwing in a few seal impressions along the way(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wrestling promoter and novice cook James hosts the second night in Hull, and he hopes to wow his guests with a Mexican-themed party complete with a wrestling match as the evening's entertainment. However, his costume requirements, and his spicy food, may be too much for some of the attendees(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wedding stationery entrepreneur Katrena hosts the third dinner party in Hull, and hopes to impress her guests with a cruise-ship-themed night featuring a pink strawberry soup starter, a sparkling lemon iceberg dessert and towel-folding entertainment(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mum-of-three Natalie hopes that her choice of good quality produce can help her to impress her guests on the fourth night of the competition in Hull, East Yorkshire. However, the evening does not get off to the best start when community manager George wrongly guesses that she is the grandmother of her two-year-old daughter, and entrepreneur Katrena is insulted by one of the host's questions(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final dinner party in Hull and the turn of community manager George to entertain the guests. Having spent the week heavily criticising everyone else's cooking, the host faces great scrutiny with his menu of simple yet tasty favourites. It remains to be seen whether his egg drop soup and flambeed crepes Suzettes live up to expectations, before the winner of the cash prize is revealed(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks into the past of Edinburgh's Palace of Holyroodhouse, which is located close to the dormant volcano Arthur's Seat. Events in the royal residence, one of the smallest palaces in Britain, have determined the futures of England, France and Scotland, and it was the last place in which a royal prince challenged the right of an English king to sit on the throne. Fiona also examines objects including the Darnley jewel, which features numerous hidden messages, and the needlework of a doomed queen(n)

programme.title: Silk
programme.desc: Martha defends a professional footballer accused of assaulting another player, but because the incident was witnessed by thousands of spectators in the stadium, it seems an impossible task. With her client reluctant to co-operate, she must gain his trust to discover a secret he's determined to keep hidden. Up against Clive in court, can the barrister convince the sportsman to help himself? Meanwhile, a resignation sends shock waves through the chambers and Billy takes steps to ensure the future of Shoe Lane - with or without him(n)

programme.title: Heartless
programme.desc: A photographer documents the gangland conflicts plaguing the council estate he lives on, and comes to realise the violence is not random, but created by unseen demonic creatures. His investigation into the supernatural leads to him being tricked into selling his soul. Horror, starring Jim Sturgess, Timothy Spall, Clemence Poesy and Noel Clarke(n)

programme.title: The Flowers of War
programme.desc: An American drifter arrives in Nanjing in 1937, just as Japanese forces invade. Hiding out in a Western-run church, he poses as a priest, but ends up adopting his role and providing shelter for orphans and courtesans as carnage unfolds in the city. Drama, starring Christian Bale and Ni Ni. In Nanjing dialect, English, Japanese, Mandarin and Shanghainese(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Routes with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The actor and five companions follow the route thousands of medieval pilgrims took from Holywell, Flintshire, to St Davids in Pembrokeshire. They forage for food, face fast-flowing rivers and sometimes walk barefoot in sackcloth as they come to understand the lengths people in the Middle Ages went to for their religious beliefs. The travellers also encounter grand manor houses, ancient churches and relics, while Griff explores how the British holiday has its roots in the observance of holy days(n)

programme.title: Posh Neighbours at War
programme.desc: Documentary looking at disputes between residents in upmarket areas of London.
